mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.2% suggests deep easing regime
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 150,925 thousand suggests near-peak employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000 persons, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) below the prior 3-month average signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below 104.0 index points signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5 points month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining rising housing starts (HOUST > 1,800 thousand units) and rising retail sales (RSAFS > 670,000 million dollars) indicates broad-based demand strength across residential and consumer sectors, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both proxies to signal simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,769 thousand units, RSAFS at 665,726 million dollars
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across housing and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances where both HOUST > 1,800 and RSAFS > 670,000, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 3.8% combined with a decline in payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both labor indicators to weaken simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.6%, PAYEMS at 150,925 thousand
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp and PAYEMS declines, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate regime (FEDFUNDS < 1.0%), a month-over-month increase in CPI inflation above 0.3% signals unexpected inflation that forces future monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional and inactive when rates are elevated.
regime_hint: low-rate monetary easing with unexpected inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL monthly growth > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) below the prior 4-quarter average signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 19,806.29 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
